stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers', 'llama', and 'text-generation'.
This indicates continued industry interest and evaluation of Meta's latest large language model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The content points to the ongoing community interest and benchmarking of Meta's Llama family of models.

stories.title: Man sues Florida cops over arrest spurred by "93% match" in facial recognition
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: A lawsuit challenges the use of facial recognition technology by Florida police.
The arrest in question was reportedly spurred by a '93% match' from an AI system.
The legal action argues that relying on error-prone AI substitutes for proper investigation.

stories.title: Google DeepMind releases DiffusionGemma, a model that runs local AI 4x faster
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google DeepMind has released DiffusionGemma, a new AI model.
The model is notable for enabling local AI execution up to 4x faster.
It demonstrates that diffusion techniques, typically used for images, can significantly accelerate text generation.

stories.title: Nobody needs AI to search the Internet, court says in ruling against Google
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: A German court ruled against Google regarding its AI search features.
The ruling suggests that AI is not necessary for traditional internet searching.
This legal setback could significantly impact the trajectory of the AI search industry.

stories.title: Which Models Are Our Models Built On? Auditing Invisible Dependencies in Modern LLMs
stories.author: Sewon Min
bullet_points: Modern LLM training relies heavily on complex, often undocumented, dependencies on other models for data generation and filtering.
These dependencies are recursive and fragmented across various public artifacts, making full traceability extremely difficult for humans.
The paper introduces 'ModSleuth,' a tool designed to audit and trace these invisible, complex dependency structures within LLMs.

stories.title: German court ruling declares Google's AI Overviews are Google's own words and makes it liable for false answers
stories.author: the-decoder.com via gspr
bullet_points: A German court ruled that Google's AI Overviews are considered Google's own generated content.
The ruling establishes Google's liability for false or inaccurate information provided by its AI Overviews.
This decision signals increasing legal scrutiny and regulatory risk for generative AI models globally.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face.
The model is categorized for text generation tasks, indicating its primary function in NLP applications.
It is associated with key ML technologies like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the modern LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The pipeline indicates the model is being used for text generation tasks.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now integrates MLX, Apple's native machine learning framework.
This integration aims to provide the fastest performance for running Ollama on Apple Silicon.
The feature is currently available in a preview release.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and speed.
The core value proposition is connecting communication tools to local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: Anthropic apologizes for invisible Claude Fable guardrails
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Anthropic apologized for implementing hidden guardrails on its new AI model, Claude Fable 5.
These undisclosed restrictions were found to be undermining researchers and competitors using the model.
The company is committing to greater transparency regarding when and how these restrictions are applied.

stories.title: FACTR 2: Learning External Force Sensing for Commodity Robot Arms Improves Policy Learning
stories.author: Deepak Pathak
bullet_points: Introduces NEXT, a data-driven method to estimate external joint torques in robot arms without dedicated, costly force sensors.
The method is highly efficient, training in just one minute using only ten minutes of free-motion data.
This advancement enables force-feedback teleoperation and improves policy learning for commodity (low-cost) robot arms.

stories.title: DIRECT: When and Where Should You Allocate Test-Time Compute in Embodied Planners?
stories.author: Marco Pavone
bullet_points: The paper addresses the current trend of scaling test-time compute in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) used for embodied planning.
It finds that simply increasing compute leads to increased latency and resource usage (FLOPs, tokens) with diminishing returns.
The core argument is that strategically deciding *when* and *where* to allocate compute is crucial for practical, real-world deployment of embodied agents.

stories.title: TAHOE: Text-to-SQL with Automated Hint Optimization from Experience
stories.author: Peng Li
bullet_points: Tahoe addresses the production challenges of Text-to-SQL, moving beyond prototype limitations.
The system tackles issues like strict SQL dialects, massive schemas, and evolving user needs.
It introduces an error-driven hint learning pipeline to dynamically optimize prompts, reducing reliance on costly supervised fine-tuning.

stories.title: FACTR 2: Learning External Force Sensing for Commodity Robot Arms Improves Policy Learning
stories.author: Deepak Pathak
bullet_points: Introduces NEXT, a data-driven method to estimate external joint torques in robot arms without requiring expensive dedicated force sensors.
The method is highly efficient, training in just 1 minute using only 10 minutes of free-motion data, while achieving sensor-comparable accuracy.
This advancement significantly lowers the barrier for force-feedback manipulation, enabling advanced control on low-cost robotic hardware.

stories.title: On Subquadratic Architectures: From Applications to Principles
stories.author: Sepp Hochreiter
bullet_points: Addresses the computational bottleneck of standard Transformer models by investigating subquadratic attention architectures.
Compares three leading scalable alternatives: xLSTM, Mamba-2, and Gated DeltaNet.
Evaluates these models on complex sequence tasks, including code-model pre-training and distillation.

stories.title: ALIGNBEAM : Inference-Time Alignment Transfer via Cross-Vocabulary Logit Mixing
stories.author: Vinay Kumar Sankarapu
bullet_points: Addresses the safety degradation of LLMs when fine-tuned for specific domains, where specialists become vulnerable to harmful prompts.
Introduces ALIGNBEAM, a training-free inference-time defense mechanism to enhance LLM safety.
Overcomes a key limitation of prior methods by translating anchor model logits into the target model's vocabulary, allowing cross-family model application.

stories.title: Google ADK Security: 5 Layers That Defend AI Agents From Prompt Injection
stories.author: ainaomotayo
bullet_points: Addresses a critical security vulnerability in AI agents: Prompt Injection.
Details Google ADK's multi-layered defense mechanism against such attacks.
Highlights real-world risks, exemplified by an unapproved financial transaction caused by a poisoned tool response.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings improved performance and expanded GGUF model compatibility.
The update enhances Ollama's MLX engine specifically for Apple silicon.
This allows Ollama to support a broader range of models across diverse hardware configurations.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Introduction of NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, offering an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these personal AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It integrates native support for Ollama, facilitating local LLM execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or config files.

stories.title: Image generation (experimental)
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports local image generation on macOS.
This feature allows users to run image generation models entirely on their local machines.
Support for Windows and Linux platforms is planned for future releases.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model execution and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: Boox’s new e-reader could replace your Kindle, Kobo, and digital notepad
stories.author: Andrew Liszewski
bullet_points: Boox has released the Go 6 (Gen II) e-reader, expanding its functionality to include note-taking capabilities.
The device maintains a 6-inch, 300PPI E Ink screen but receives a RAM upgrade from 2GB to 3GB.
A key addition is support for Boox's InkSense Plus stylus, enhancing its digital notepad features.

stories.title: "This cannot continue": Xbox leaders lay out "hard truths" behind sagging brand
stories.author: Kyle Orland
bullet_points: Xbox leadership has issued a candid self-assessment regarding the state of the gaming division.
The report suggests the brand is facing significant challenges, described as a 'crisis'.
The article details the 'hard truths' identified internally by Microsoft's gaming executives.

stories.title: Reroute, Don't Remove: Recoverable Visual Token Routing for Vision-Language Models
stories.author: Yu-Lun Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the computational bottleneck (attention and KV-cache) in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) caused by high numbers of visual tokens.
Proposes 'Recoverable Visual Token Routing' as an alternative to existing 'rank-and-remove' methods.
Highlights the flaw in current methods: permanently discarding tokens is fragile because token importance evolves across the decoder's depth.

stories.title: Redesign Mixture-of-Experts Routers with Manifold Power Iteration
stories.author: Yankai Lin
bullet_points: The research addresses a fundamental design challenge in Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models: improving the router component.
The router's role is to determine which subset of experts is activated based on input token similarity.
The paper proposes using Manifold Power Iteration to better encode expert information into the router's representative vectors.

stories.title: ATLAS: Active Theory Learning for Automated Science
stories.author: Kevin J. Miller
bullet_points: Introduces ATLAS, an active learning framework designed to automate scientific discovery.
ATLAS focuses on generating interpretable, mechanistic behavioral models from data.
The framework iteratively generates hypotheses using an ensemble of sparse neural networks (Disentangled RNNs).

stories.title: APPO: Agentic Procedural Policy Optimization
stories.author: Xiangxiang Chu
bullet_points: The research addresses a limitation in current agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) methods regarding fine-grained credit assignment.
It focuses on two key areas: determining optimal branching points within agent workflows and accurately attributing credit after those branches.
The work aims to improve the understanding and performance of LLM agents that utilize multi-turn tool-use.

stories.title: Illumination-Robust Camera-Based Heart-Rate Estimation for Physiological Sensing in Robots
stories.author: Torbjörn E. M. Nordling
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge of illumination variation in remote Photoplethysmography (rPPG) for heart-rate estimation.
It introduces an end-to-end spatial-temporal transformer framework to improve robustness in non-contact physiological sensing.
The work is relevant to the development of advanced service, social, and assistive robots that require human physiological awareness.

stories.title: Context-Driven Incremental Compression for Multi-Turn Dialogue Generation
stories.author: Lei Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the computational burden of long dialogue histories in conversational AI due to growing attention costs.
Identifies limitations in current context compression methods, specifically lacking cross-turn memory sharing and revision capabilities.
Proposes a new context-driven incremental compression technique to improve efficiency and fidelity in multi-turn dialogue generation.

stories.title: Redesign Mixture-of-Experts Routers with Manifold Power Iteration
stories.author: Yankai Lin
bullet_points: The paper addresses a fundamental design challenge in Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) models: how to effectively design the router component.
The proposed solution involves using Manifold Power Iteration to redesign the MoE routers.
The goal is to ensure router rows better encode the expert matrix to improve token-expert affinity determination.

stories.title: ATLAS: Active Theory Learning for Automated Science
stories.author: Kevin J. Miller
bullet_points: Introduces ATLAS, an active learning framework designed to automate scientific discovery.
ATLAS focuses on generating interpretable, mechanistic behavioral models using data-driven methods.
The framework iteratively generates hypotheses using an ensemble of sparse neural networks (Disentangled RNNs).

stories.title: APPO: Agentic Procedural Policy Optimization
stories.author: Xiangxiang Chu
bullet_points: Addresses a limitation in agentic Reinforcement Learning (RL) where credit assignment is too coarse (e.g., at tool-call boundaries).
Proposes a study focusing on two key areas: determining optimal branching points and refining credit assignment post-branching.
Contributes to the ongoing advancement of large language model agents with multi-turn tool-use capabilities.

stories.title: UniIntervene: Agentic Intervention for Efficient Real-World Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Ziwei Wang
bullet_points: Introduces UniIntervene, a new agentic intervention model for Human-in-the-loop Reinforcement Learning (HiL-RL).
Addresses the major limitation of current HiL-RL: high labor costs due to frequent human corrections during policy exploration.
Aims to improve the efficiency and scalability of real-world robotic manipulation by automating intervention.

stories.title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
stories.author: Jingren Zhou
bullet_points: The research addresses the bottleneck in Reinforcement Learning (RL) training pipelines, specifically during the rollout stage.
It focuses on Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) and its limitations in RL training, where acceptance rates degrade.
The authors introduce 'Bebop,' a systematic study aimed at improving MTP performance for accelerating LLM post-training.

stories.title: Anatomy of Post-Training: Using Interpretability to Characterize Data and Shape the Learning Signal
stories.author: Ekdeep Singh Lubana
bullet_points: The paper addresses the lack of visibility into how data shapes language model behavior during post-training.
Current post-training relies too heavily on scalar rewards, obscuring the diverse learning signals present in the data.
The research proposes using interpretability techniques to inspect preference datasets *before* optimization to mitigate issues like spurious correlations and sycophancy.

stories.title: Context-Driven Incremental Compression for Multi-Turn Dialogue Generation
stories.author: Lei Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the computational bottleneck in dialogue systems caused by growing dialogue history.
Proposes a context-driven incremental compression method to manage long conversational context.
Highlights limitations in existing compressors, such as lack of cross-turn memory sharing and revision capabilities.

stories.title: Doc-to-Atom: Learning to Compile and Compose Memory Atoms
stories.author: Srinivas Chappidi
bullet_points: Addresses the quadratic complexity and memory/speed issues of attention in LLMs when handling long input sequences.
Proposes 'Doc-to-Atom,' a method related to context distillation, to efficiently process document-level context.
The work aims to improve upon existing methods (like Doc-to-LoRA) by addressing the limitations of monolithic adapters for varied queries.

stories.title: Verifiable Environments Are LEGO Bricks: Recursive Composition for Reasoning Generalization
stories.author: Dayiheng Liu
bullet_points: The research addresses the limitation of scaling reasoning generalization in LLMs using Reinforcement Learning (RL) with verifiable environments.
It introduces RACES, a method for recursive, automated composition of environments to overcome linear scaling limits of manual construction.
The work aims to enhance the reasoning capabilities of LLMs by providing a scalable way to build complex, verifiable training environments.

stories.title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
stories.author: Jingren Zhou
bullet_points: The research addresses the bottleneck of slow rollout stages in Reinforcement Learning (RL) training for Large Language Models (LLMs).
It focuses on Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) and its limitations, specifically the degradation of acceptance rates during RL training.
The paper introduces 'Bebop,' a systematic study aimed at improving MTP performance to accelerate LLM post-training.

stories.title: Claw-SWE-Bench: A Benchmark for Evaluating OpenClaw-style Agent Harnesses on Coding Tasks
stories.author: Yu Wang
bullet_points: Introduces Claw-SWE-Bench, a new benchmark designed to evaluate open-source agent harnesses (like OpenClaw) on coding tasks.
Addresses a key limitation in existing benchmarks (like SWE-bench) where generic agents fail to meet the strict environment requirements for scoring.
Provides a standardized, multilingual protocol to allow fair comparison of different heterogeneous agent harnesses.

stories.title: Pokémon Go Scans Trained the Navigation Tech for Military Drones
stories.author: vrganj
bullet_points: Pokémon Go's underlying technology was repurposed for military drone navigation.
This highlights the crossover potential between consumer mobile gaming tech and advanced defense applications.
The story suggests a practical application of AI/ML learned from mass-market data to specialized military hardware.

stories.title: Workers are spending over 6 hours a week botsitting AI, fueling job frustration
stories.author: ZeidJ
bullet_points: Workers are dedicating significant time (over 6 hours weekly) to 'botsitting' AI systems.
This trend is directly contributing to growing job frustration among employees.
The story highlights a current, practical challenge in the integration and maintenance of AI tools.

stories.title: CVE-2026-45257: LPE in FreeBSD via kTLS-RX
stories.author: bumsrake.de via dgst
bullet_points: A critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-45257) has been identified in FreeBSD.
The vulnerability is classified as a Local Privilege Escalation (LPE).
The flaw resides within the kTLS-RX component of the operating system.
